evals.name: tlv-header-with-bit-field
evals.prompt: I have a scaffolded Go package at ./tlv/ with types.go/decoder.go/encoder.go stubs and a SPEC.md describing the TLV1 format. Implement the Header structure end-to-end: the Magic/Version/Flags/Reserved fields described in SPEC.md's Field Definitions section, including the Flags bit field (COMPRESSED / ENCRYPTED / SIGNED). Update File to hold a Header. I want decode tests with hex literals, encode tests, a round-trip test, and a failure-path test that asserts the FieldError → OffsetError → leaf chain. Don't read the whole SPEC.md — slice it.
evals.expected_output: tlv/types.go gains a Header struct and Flags bit-field constants; tlv/decoder.go gains readHeader funneling errors through d.wrapErr; tlv/encoder.go gains writeHeader funneling errors through e.wrapErr; tests cover decode/encode/round-trip/failure-path and pass under `go test -race`.

assertions.text: types.go defines a Header struct with Magic, Version, Flags, and Reserved fields
types.go defines bit-field mask constants for COMPRESSED, ENCRYPTED, and SIGNED on the Flags byte
the File struct in types.go references Header
decoder.go defines a readHeader method on the decoder type
encoder.go defines a writeHeader method on the encoder type
every new error site in decoder.go and encoder.go uses d.wrapErr / e.wrapErr (no direct construction of FieldError or OffsetError outside the helpers)
decoder_test.go has a happy-path test that decodes a hex byte literal containing the TLV1 magic and asserts Header field values
decoder_test.go has a failure-path test asserting errors.Is on a leaf sentinel AND errors.As on FieldError with a header-related Field path
encoder_test.go has a happy-path test that encodes a Header and asserts the output bytes match a hex literal
encoder_test.go has a round-trip test that runs Encode → Decode and asserts require.Equal on the original
all new tests call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
tests use github.com/stretchr/testify/require
go test -race ./... exits cleanly
no leftover _spec_*.md or _context_*.md scratch files in the package directory
no copy of SPEC.md content was written to a scratch file in the package (e.g. _spec_structures.md should not exist)

evals.name: tlv-record-variable-length
evals.prompt: Implement the Record structure for the TLV1 format in ./tlv/. Each Record has a Type (uint8), a Length (uint16, big-endian), and a Value of Length bytes (per SPEC.md's Field Definitions). Add an exported RecordType enum with constants for STRING, INT, BLOB, NESTED matching the encoding table. Update File to hold a slice of Records. Tests should cover: a typical STRING record, an empty record (Length=0), an INT record (Length=8), and a round-trip per record type. Don't load the full SPEC.md.
evals.expected_output: tlv/types.go gains Record struct and RecordType enum with String() method; tlv/decoder.go gains readRecord with proper variable-length handling (length prefix → ReadFull); tlv/encoder.go gains writeRecord; tests cover empty/typical/INT records and round-trip; `go test -race` passes.

assertions.text: types.go defines a Record struct with Type, Length, and Value fields
types.go defines a RecordType enum with constants for STRING (0x01), INT (0x02), BLOB (0x03), and NESTED (0x04)
types.go defines a String() method on the RecordType type
the File struct in types.go has a Records slice field
decoder.go defines a readRecord method that reads the length prefix and then the value bytes
encoder.go defines a writeRecord method
decoder.go uses io.ReadFull (not r.Read) to read the variable-length Value
decoder_test.go has a test for a STRING record with a non-empty value
decoder_test.go has a test for an empty record (Length = 0)
encoder_test.go has a round-trip test for at least one record type
every new error site uses d.wrapErr / e.wrapErr
all new tests call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
go test -race ./... exits cleanly
no scratch copy of SPEC.md content (e.g. _spec_structures.md) was written to the package

evals.name: tlv-trailer-with-crc32
evals.prompt: Implement the Trailer for the TLV1 format in ./tlv/ — a 4-byte big-endian CRC32 (IEEE polynomial) covering every byte preceding the trailer (see SPEC.md's 'Trailer' field definition and 'Checksums and Integrity' section). On decode, compute the running CRC32 as bytes are read and compare it to the trailer; on mismatch, return a typed error like ErrChecksumMismatch wrapped through wrapErr. On encode, compute and write the CRC32. Update File to hold a Trailer (or expose CRC32 directly). Add tests for the happy path AND for a CRC mismatch. Don't load the full SPEC.md.
evals.expected_output: tlv/types.go gains a Trailer struct (or CRC32 field on File) plus an ErrChecksumMismatch sentinel; tlv/decoder.go computes the CRC32 alongside reading and verifies it; tlv/encoder.go computes and writes the CRC32; tests verify both happy path and mismatch detection; `go test -race` passes.

assertions.text: types.go either defines a Trailer struct or adds a CRC32 field to File
types.go defines an ErrChecksumMismatch sentinel (or equivalent typed error)
decoder.go imports hash/crc32 and uses the IEEE polynomial table
encoder.go imports hash/crc32 and uses the IEEE polynomial table
decoder.go compares the computed CRC32 against the trailer value and returns an error on mismatch
encoder.go writes the computed CRC32 as the final 4 bytes
decoder_test.go has a test that decodes a payload with a valid CRC32 successfully
decoder_test.go has a test that decodes a payload with a deliberately corrupted CRC32 and asserts errors.Is(err, ErrChecksumMismatch)
encoder_test.go has a round-trip test that runs Encode → Decode and verifies the decoded value equals the original
the CRC mismatch error funnels through d.wrapErr (FieldError → OffsetError → ErrChecksumMismatch)
all new tests call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
go test -race ./... exits cleanly
no scratch copy of SPEC.md content was written to the package
